agent.name: __eval_jsonout
agent.taskDescription: Create a JSON object representing a book with these exact fields: title (string), author (string), year (number), genres (array of strings). Use any real book you like.
agent.systemPrompt: You MUST output a valid JSON object in your response. No markdown code fences, no explanation text before or after — just the raw JSON object.

judgeCriteria.name: Valid JSON
judgeCriteria.rubric: Is the output a valid, parseable JSON object containing all four required fields (title, author, year, genres)? Is the year a number (not string)? Is genres an array?

agent.name: __eval_mdtable
agent.taskDescription: Create a Markdown table with 3 columns (Language, Year Created, Creator) and exactly 4 rows of data for programming languages. Include the header row and separator row.
agent.systemPrompt: Output ONLY the Markdown table. No explanation before or after. Use standard pipe-delimited Markdown table syntax.

judgeCriteria.name: Valid Markdown table
judgeCriteria.rubric: Is this a well-formed Markdown table with a header row, separator row (dashes), and exactly 4 data rows? Can it be rendered correctly by a Markdown parser?

agent.name: __eval_csvout
agent.taskDescription: Create a CSV file with headers: Name, City, Favorite Quote. Include exactly 3 rows of data. At least one row must have a comma in the Favorite Quote field (requiring proper CSV quoting). At least one row must have a quote character in a field (requiring proper escaping).
agent.systemPrompt: Output ONLY valid CSV text. No explanation. Follow RFC 4180: fields with commas or quotes must be enclosed in double quotes, and literal quotes within fields must be escaped as double-double-quotes.

judgeCriteria.name: Valid CSV with escaping
judgeCriteria.rubric: Is this valid CSV per RFC 4180? Does it have a header row and 3 data rows? Is at least one field with a comma properly quoted? Is at least one field with a quote character properly escaped with double-double-quotes?
